Petition To Continue Court Supervision Of Trusts And For Reimbursement Of Fees And Costs To Attorneys
Off Calendar Dec-27-2010 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce Feder for and with Robin Indelicato. Mary Pryce for and with William Buda. Margaret Laughlin Martin for and with Claudia Johnson.

Mr. Feder stated that they were requesting to retain the Trust under Court supervision for the reasons set forth on the record, that they want the opportunity to review the Trustee's conduct if needed, that things had started off rough, that they want a forum for any significant action, and that the difference of the cost to the Trust would be minimal. Ms. Martin argues that in 2008 that they thought one year of supervision would allow a chance for Mrs. Indelicato to get to know Ms. Johnson, that her client is a private professional fiduciary, that there has been no rancor between her client and Ms. Indelicato, addressed the other Trusts, and stated that Ms. Indelicato is entitled to notice on Sub-Trusts B and C, and that her client disputes the allegations regarding the accountings and the lack of contact. In addition, Ms. Martin stated that her client has accounted as required, that it is their position to notify the parties of their intention, that they only planned to bring disputed matters before the Court, and that they are objecting because there are no grounds. Mr. Feder stated that his client worked for years with her Mother, that part of the agreement is that the Trust would be under Court supervision for at least one year, that there would be accountings on all three Trusts, that the accountings would be ongoing, that the information that was supposed to be conveyed hasn't been as it was scheduled, and that Court-Appointed Counsel has not objected to the petition. The Court indicated that it was leaning towards denying the petition which would include the fees. Mr. Feder presented argument in support of his fees. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Brown.
